headline: Exxon, Occidental, and Cheniere Stocks Jump. Three Reasons Why.
summary: Oil-and-gas company stocks gained early Monday amid escalating tensions in the Middle East and as investors positioned themselves for the possibility of extended supply disruptions.  Oil exploration and production companies  Exxon Chevron  and  Occidental Petroleum  were all up in premarket trading—by 0.7%, 0.7%, and 1.2%, respectively.  Natural gas companies had a similarly strong start to the week.

headline: Greenland’s Untested Oil Basin Could Be the Next Big Discovery
summary: Greenland Energy Company, led by Texas oil veteran Robert Price, is preparing to drill the Jameson Land basin in Greenland's east coast, a strategically important, untested Arctic petroleum system that geologists believe could hold over 13 billion barrels of recoverable oil.

headline: Exxon Mobil Shifts Supply Routes As Investors Weigh Valuation And Growth
summary: Exxon Mobil began shipping gasoline to Australia for the first time, responding to supply disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The company is also considering a return to Venezuela, with any move depending on investment terms and operating conditions. For investors watching NYSE:XOM, these moves sit against a backdrop of robust recent longer term share price performance. The stock last closed at $150.44, with a 1 year return of 39.2% and a...
